title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false
input_row.description: Disable anonymous requests to the API server.
input_row.rationale: When enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests. These requests are then served by the API server. You should rely on authentication to authorize access and disallow anonymous requests. If you are using RBAC authorization, it is generally considered reasonable to allow anonymous access to the API Server for health checks and discovery purposes, and hence this recommendation is not scored. However, you should consider whether anonymous discovery is an acceptable risk for your purposes. Impact: Anonymous requests will be rejected.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false. Alternative Audit kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command} {"\n"}{end}' | grep '\--anonymous- auth' | grep -i false If the exit code is '1', then the control isn't present / failed
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the below parameter. --anonymous-auth=false Default Value: By default, anonymous access is enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authentication/#anonymous-requests

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_anonymous_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command} {{"\\n"}}{end}'` to retrieve the command-line arguments used to start the kube-apiserver pods.
- Specifically, check for the presence of the `--anonymous-auth` flag in the output.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - If the output contains `--anonymous-auth=false`, the control is compliant (Pass).
  - If the output does not contain `--anonymous-auth=false` or if the flag is absent, the control is non-compliant (Fail).
- This check ensures that anonymous requests to the API server are disabled, aligning with the security best practices outlined in the CIS Kubernetes Benchmark.
